sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any
necessary amendments with artist Julie W. Chang for an amount not to exceed
$150,000 for a public art commission of a mosaic mural on the 18’-5” wide by 10’ tall
interior lobby wall located in the Willard Clubhouse at the southeast corner of Willard
Park at 2720 Hillegass Avenue, between Derby Street and Stuart Street.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The $150,000 contract will be funded through Measure T1 bond funds (Fund Code 511-
21-208-252-0000-000-446-612990) in Fiscal Year 2025.
sections.current_situation:
Artist Julie W. Chang was selected through a competitive process to create a
permanent artwork for the interior lobby wall of the Willard Clubhouse located at the
southeast corner of Willard Park. The goal of the project is to enhance a visitor’s
experience of Willard Park and Clubhouse with a visual amenity; to respond to the site
thematically; and to appeal to a wide age range of Willard Park and Clubhouse visitors.
Julie W. Chang’s proposal Enchanted Forest draws inspiration from Berkeley’s flora and
fauna and the history of Willard Park as a beloved gathering space. With the intent of
highlighting diverse voices and perspectives, symbols mingle and collide to abstraction,
inviting multiple interpretations and opportunities for delight and discovery for children
and viewers of all ages. Located on the interior lobby wall, this 18’-5” wide by 10’ tall
“woven” mosaic will serve as a celebration of community in harmony with our natural
environment (Figure 1).

sections.background:
On January 18, 2023, the Civic Arts Commission approved the Willard Park Clubhouse
Public Art Project Plan, which called for the commissioning of a mosaic artwork for the
18’-5” wide by 10’ tall interior lobby wall located at the entrance to the Willard
Clubhouse as follows: M/S/C (Passmore/Dhesi) to approve the Willard Clubhouse
Public Art Project Plan. Vote: Ayes — Anno, Blecher, Bullwinkel, Covarrubias, Dhesi,
Ibarra, Ozol, Passmore, Woo; Nays — None; Abstain — None; Absent — None.
A Request for Qualifications for this artwork commission was released on February 13,
2024 and was open through April 9, 2024. A selection panel comprised of arts
professionals reviewed the qualifications of the 135 applicants and recommended 28
applications to be presented to the Willard Clubhouse Public Art Project selection panel.
The selection panel of seven people was comprised of three Bay Area arts
professionals, a Civic Arts Commissioner, a Parks, Recreation and Waterfront
Commissioner, a member of the clubhouse’s architecture team, and a community
representative. This panel reviewed the qualifications of the 28 artists on the shortlist
and recommended three finalists to develop detailed conceptual site-specific proposals.
Once the proposals were received, staff conducted extensive community outreach to
elicit the public’s responses to the three detailed proposals. A “Berkeley Considers”
survey that invited respondents to provide feedback on each of the proposals was
hosted on the City’s website from October 21 to November 11, 2024 and was widely
distributed through a citywide community message yielding 535 survey responses.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 3 of 4
Contract: Julie W. Chang Public Artwork for Willard Clubhouse CONSENT CALENDAR
January 21, 2025
On November 18, 2024, the selection panel was reconvened to consider the finalists
sections.rationale:
The proposal by Julie W. Chang was selected based upon the following criteria:
aesthetic quality; appropriateness of the proposed artwork for the site and project goals;
demonstrated feasibility of the preliminary proposal and the proposal budget; and
demonstrated maintainability and durability of the design, materials, fabrication and
proposed installation method for the artwork.
sections.alternatives:
The selection panel considered two other artistic proposals for the clubhouse wall, but
ultimately selected Julie W. Chang’s proposal Enchanted Forest for advancement.
CONTACT PERSON
Mark Salinas, Office of Economic Development, (510) 981-7538

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT WITH JULIE W. CHANG TO CREATE A PUBLIC ARTWORK ON THE
LOBBY WALL OF THE WILLARD CLUBHOUSE
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley’s Arts and Culture Plan calls for the enhancement of
Berkeley’s environment by placing temporary and permanent public art in public spaces
throughout the city; and
WHEREAS, the Civic Arts Commission approved the Willard Clubhouse Public Art
Project Plan on January 18, 2023, which called for the commissioning of a permanent
mosaic artwork for the interior lobby wall of the Willard Clubhouse located at the
southeast corner of Willard Park; and
WHEREAS, funding for the artist contract for the mosaic artwork at Willard Park is
included in the budget of the Measure T1 bond funds (Fund 511) for Fiscal Year 2025;
and
WHERES, a Request for Qualifications from professional practicing artists was released
on February 13, 2024 and 135 applications were received; and
WHEREAS, a selection panel comprised of arts professionals reviewed the
qualifications of the 135 applicants and recommended 28 applications to be presented
to the Willard Clubhouse Public Art selection panel comprised of seven people including
three Bay Area arts professionals, a Civic Arts Commissioner, a Parks, Recreation and
Waterfront Commissioner, a member of the project’s architect team, and a community
representative; and
WHEREAS, three finalists were selected by the panel and invited to develop site-
specific public art project proposals; and
WHEREAS, the selection panel reconvened to evaluate the three proposals and
selected the proposal by Julie W. Chang for recommendation to the Civic Arts
Commission; and
WHEREAS, the Civic Arts Commission approved Julie W. Chang for a permanent
mosaic artwork for the interior lobby wall of the Willard Clubhouse at its regula
